title: brachyelytrum silvaticum
intro: Brachyelytrum silvaticum, also known as the woodland false mannagrass, is a type of cool-season perennial grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional herbal preparations, Brachyelytrum silvaticum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
